message:
Update beads tracker: close bd-3y9i, bd-t0hz; note bd-b3h0 blockers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3uz.8","title":"Activate MermaidGuardReport and MermaidDegradationPlan across pipeline and surfaces","description":"Activate `MermaidGuardReport` and `MermaidDegradationPlan` end-to-end so configuration contracts in fm-core become runtime reality.

Scope:
- Populate guard/degradation structures during parse/layout/render.
- Ensure CLI/WASM surfaces receive and expose these structures.
- Add deterministic tests validating transitions between normal and degraded tiers.
- Add clear user-facing diagnostics explaining which safeguards fired and why.

Why this matters:
The core data contracts exist; they must be wired into the pipeline to deliver promised resilience.

Acceptance criteria:
- GuardReport and DegradationPlan are non-stub and populated in real runs.
- Transition paths are fully covered by unit/integration tests.
- Diagnostics include trigger conditions, chosen operator set, and resulting quality tier.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.569575745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T18:31:09.642607669Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T18:31:09.642091469Z","close_reason":"MermaidGuardReport and MermaidDegradationPlan now fully activated across pipeline and surfaces. Guard report populated with real complexity, limit, and budget data from parse/layout/render. Degradation plan computed via deterministic operator library (compute_degradation_plan). SVG renderer consumes reduce_decoration, target_fidelity. Terminal renderer consumes force_glyph_mode, collapse_clusters, hide_labels, target_fidelity. CLI and WASM surfaces wire degradation to all render backends. 29+ tests cover operator selection, renderer activation, and noop defaults.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","fm-core","graceful-degradation","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2b4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.750618319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.569575745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:26.812781326Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:26.896447947Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:26.985011906Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.662533146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":55,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Future-self rationale:  and  already exist in fm-core contracts; this task converts those definitions into live runtime behavior. Do not treat this as optional polish. It is the main reliability bridge for overloaded-machine scenarios.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:39Z"},{"id":58,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction note: `MermaidGuardReport` and `MermaidDegradationPlan` are explicit fm-core contracts that must be populated by live parser/layout/render execution. This task is the bridge from schema-only definitions to runtime-enforced graceful degradation and should be treated as core correctness work, not optional polish.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:58:06Z"},{"id":66,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3uz.9","title":"Add structured pressure/degradation diagnostics schema across CLI and WASM","description":"Define and emit structured pressure/degradation diagnostics so automated systems can reason about runtime behavior under stress.

Scope:
- Extend diagnostics schema with budget usage, pressure tier, degradation operators, and confidence.
- Emit in `fm-cli validate --format json` and trace bundle outputs.
- Expose equivalent data in WASM APIs for browser telemetry and debuggability.
- Add schema versioning and backward-compatible evolution rules.

Why this matters:
Graceful degradation is only operationally useful if it is machine-observable.

Acceptance criteria:
- Schema is documented and versioned.
- CLI and WASM emit consistent pressure diagnostics.
- Trace replay reproduces observed degradation decisions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.661368152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T18:33:25.695905007Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T18:33:25.695493376Z","close_reason":"Structured pressure/degradation diagnostics schema now emitted across CLI and WASM. ValidateResult and RenderResult include: budget usage per stage (parse/layout/render_used_ms), degradation plan fields (target_fidelity, reduce_decoration, simplify_routing, hide_labels, collapse_clusters, force_glyph_mode), pressure signals (source, tier, score_permille, telemetry_available), and decision ledger JSONL. Schema version tracked via MermaidObservabilityIds.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","schema","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2b4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.931674026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.661368152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.839676013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":88,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3v97","title":"Make pie chart layout dimensions content-adaptive","description":"## What
Replace hardcoded pie chart dimensions (radius=140, label_radius=+60, cx/cy offsets) with values computed from the number of slices, label dimensions, and available space.

## Why
The pie chart layout in layout_diagram_pie_traced() (fm-layout/src/lib.rs) uses fixed dimensions:
- `radius = 140.0` — does not adapt to slice count
- `label_radius = radius + 60.0` — does not adapt to label length
- `cx = radius + 80.0`, `cy = radius + 60.0` — does not center properly with varying legend widths

With many slices (>8), labels overlap each other. With very long labels, they extend beyond the diagram bounds. With few slices, the chart is unnecessarily large.

## Proposed Approach
1. Compute `radius` from: `max(100.0, min(200.0, slice_count * 20.0))`
2. Compute `label_radius` from: `radius + max_label_width * 0.5 + 20.0`
3. Compute `cx`/`cy` to center the pie within the computed bounds
4. Consider adding `PieLayoutConfig` struct to LayoutConfig with `min_radius`, `max_radius`, `label_padding` fields

## Acceptance Criteria
- Pie charts with 2-3 slices are compact
- Pie charts with 15+ slices are larger with readable labels
- Labels never overlap (or at least minimize overlap with angle-based spreading)
- No regression for typical (3-8 slice) pie charts","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:59.779875169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:39:07.107523898Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:39:07.107000247Z","close_reason":"Pie radius now scales with slice count: base_radius=100+8*n, clamped to [80,220]. Label radius and center computed from radius. Replaces fixed 140/60/80/60.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-3y9i","title":"Add live demo verification evidence to README deployment section","description":"## Problem
README claims "Live Demo: https://dicklesworthstone.github.io/frankenmermaid/" and describes post-deploy verification, but doesn't include evidence of the demo actually working. Verified 2026-04-09: demo IS working — comprehensive showcase with 80 examples, live editor, style studio, diagnostics, determinism checker.

## Scope
1. Add a CI step (or post-deploy check in pages.yml) that curls the demo URL and verifies HTTP 200 + expected content.
2. Optionally: add a badge to README showing demo status (e.g., using shields.io endpoint badge).
3. Document the demo's features in README (currently just a bare URL link).

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI verifies demo is reachable after deploy.
- README deployment section mentions what the demo includes (showcase, playground, diagnostics).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:26:08.260566231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:05.751075061Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3y9i","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:05.750527344Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-4gmf","title":"Requirement: Implement risk levels, verification methods, and traceability","description":"# Requirement Diagram Improvements

## Gaps
- Risk levels (high/medium/low) parsed but not visually indicated (color coding)
- Verification methods (analysis/inspection/test/demonstration) not rendered
- Traceability matrix view not available
- Element docRef not rendered as links
- Relationship type labels not visually differentiated

## Implementation Plan
1. Apply color coding to requirement boxes based on risk level (red=high, yellow=medium, green=low)
2. Show verification method as an icon or text badge on requirement nodes
3. Render element docRef as clickable link (same as flowchart click directive)
4. Differentiate relationship types visually (satisfies=solid, contains=dashed, etc.)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:12.232116666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T18:44:24.815674826Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T18:44:24.815383936Z","close_reason":"Risk-level color coding (red/yellow/green fill based on High/Medium/Low), verification method + risk text badges in SVG nodes, requirement type header (<<requirement>>), relationship type labels (satisfies/contains/derives etc.) captured as edge labels with visual differentiation (ThickArrow for contains, DottedArrow for derives/copies/refines). CSS classes for all metadata. 5 new tests, golden snapshot updated. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","requirement"],"comments":[{"id":170,"issue_id":"bd-4gmf","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:44Z"},{"id":190,"issue_id":"bd-4gmf","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. Requirement with risk:high → verify red color coding on requirement box
2. Requirement with verifymethod:test → verify badge/icon showing verification method
3. Element with docRef:'http://...' → verify clickable link
4. Relationship 'satisfies' → verify solid arrow line
5. Relationship 'contains' → verify dashed line or different style
6. Requirement with all properties (id, text, risk, verifymethod) → verify all render","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-508m","title":"Implement sequence diagram autonumber","description":"# Sequence Autonumber

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  Alice->>Bob: First (1)
  Bob->>Alice: Second (2)
\`\`\`
- \`autonumber\` enables sequential numbering on all messages
- Numbers appear as a small circled number next to each message arrow
- Can be toggled on/off mid-diagram

## Current State
- NOT parsed. \`autonumber\` line produces a warning.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Recognize \`autonumber\` as SequenceStatement::Autonumber.
2. **IR**: Add autonumber: bool flag to IrSequenceMeta.
3. **SVG Renderer**: When autonumber is true, prepend a circled number to each message label.
4. **Tests**: Autonumber on, verify numbers appear in SVG.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:09.887912141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:02:25.665996068Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:02:25.665460955Z","close_reason":"Implemented autonumber parsing and SVG rendering. Parser recognizes 'autonumber' keyword as SequenceStatement::Autonumber, sets IrSequenceMeta.autonumber=true via IrBuilder.enable_autonumber(). SVG renderer prepends edge number to labels when autonumber is enabled. 3 parser tests added. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-508m","depends_on_id":"bd-6zd9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:26.628593830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":147,"issue_id":"bd-508m","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates

- Title (\`pie title "Title"\`) is parsed but not rendered in SVG output

@@ -365,7 +365,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-b0pc.8","title":"Improve sequence diagram fragment rendering (loop/alt/par nesting)","description":"Reality check found: Sequence diagrams are marked 'Partial' in the README. The parser comprehensively captures fragments (loop, alt, par, opt, neg), boxes, notes, activations, and 26 arrow types. But layout/rendering of fragment nesting may be incomplete.

## Scope
1. Create comprehensive test inputs with nested fragments:
   a. Simple fragment: 'loop Every minute; A->>B: ping; end'
   b. Nested alt/else: 'alt success; A->>B: ok; else failure; A->>B: err; end'
   c. Double-nested: 'loop; alt; A->>B: yes; else; A->>B: no; end; end'
   d. Par blocks: 'par; A->>B: one; and; A->>C: two; end'
   e. Fragment with box: 'box Group; participant A; participant B; end'
2. Render each to SVG and terminal, inspect output:
   a. Are fragment boundaries visually rendered (labeled rectangles)?
   b. Are nested fragments properly indented/contained?
   c. Do fragment labels (loop, alt, else, par) appear in the output?
   d. Are activation bars rendered on lifelines?
3. If fragments are NOT visually rendered:
   a. Add fragment boundary rendering to fm-render-svg: draw labeled rounded-rect for each fragment region
   b. Add fragment support to fm-render-term: draw box chars around fragment regions
   c. The LayoutExtensions.sequence_fragments (or similar) field should carry fragment geometry
4. If fragments ARE rendered but poorly:
   a. Fix nesting overlap issues
   b. Ensure label positioning doesn't collide with messages
5. Once complete: update README status from 'Partial' to 'Implemented'

## Test Plan
- Golden SVG test for each of the 5 fragment test cases above
- Assert SVG output contains 'rect' elements for fragment boundaries (not just messages)
- Assert terminal output has box-drawing characters around fragment regions
- Property test: any valid sequence input with fragments produces SVG with fragment-count rect elements

## Cross-reference
- bd-b0pc.11 (e2e smoke tests) depends on this
- No existing bead covers sequence fragment rendering specifically","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.665916008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:32:34.341943887Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:32:34.341560741Z","close_reason":"Investigation found the full pipeline already works: parser captures fragments, layout computes geometry (build_sequence_fragment_geometry), SVG renders dashed labeled rects, terminal renders box chars. Added 6 end-to-end tests confirming loop/alt/par/nested fragments render correctly. Activation bars, notes, lifecycle markers, and mirror headers also fully implemented.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","reality-check","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.8","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.665916008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b0pc.9","title":"Add PNG rasterization smoke test under --features png","description":"Reality check found: README documents PNG output via '--features png' using resvg/usvg, but there are zero tests for this feature in the CI suite.

## Scope
1. Verify the png feature compiles: cargo check -p fm-cli --features png
2. If it fails: fix Cargo.toml dependency versions for resvg/usvg, update any API changes
3. Create integration test in fm-cli/tests/ (feature-gated with #[cfg(feature = "png")]):
   a. Render 'flowchart LR; A-->B-->C' to PNG via the render pipeline
   b. Verify output file exists and is non-empty
   c. Check PNG magic bytes: first 8 bytes should be [0x89, 0x50, 0x4E, 0x47, 0x0D, 0x0A, 0x1A, 0x0A]
   d. Verify image dimensions are reasonable (> 100x100 pixels, < 10000x10000)
   e. Test with different themes: default and dark should produce different PNG content
4. Add CI job or test configuration that runs with --features png
5. If resvg/usvg are too heavy for CI, add a note about optional PNG support

## Test Plan
- cargo test -p fm-cli --features png -- png (run only PNG-specific tests)
- Assert PNG magic bytes
- Assert file size > 1KB (not empty/trivial)
- Assert multiple diagram types produce valid PNGs (flowchart + sequence at minimum)
- Log file sizes and generation times for performance baseline

## Cross-reference
- PNG feature is documented in README under 'fm-cli render' and under 'Feature Flags'
- No existing bead covers PNG testing","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.852743651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:41:50.342685190Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T04:41:50.342414718Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["png","reality-check","render","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.9","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.852743651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b2l4","title":"Harden SVG link emission against unsafe hrefs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T13:32:26.069772029Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T13:38:26.609990321Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T13:38:26.609716050Z","close_reason":"Hardened SVG link emission against unsafe hrefs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-b3h0","title":"Prepare crates.io publishing strategy for workspace crates","description":"## Problem
README admits: "frankenmermaid is not published on crates.io yet, so cargo install frankenmermaid is not a working install path today." This is honest but represents a product gap. Users who expect cargo install will be disappointed.

## Challenges
The workspace has 8 crates (fm-core, fm-parser, fm-layout, fm-render-svg, fm-render-term, fm-render-canvas, fm-wasm, fm-cli). Publishing to crates.io requires:
1. Each crate must have unique names (fm-core might conflict; may need frankenmermaid-core etc.)
2. Dependencies between crates must use crates.io versions, not path dependencies
3. The FNX git dependency (franken_networkx) must be optional and not required for the default publish
4. License, description, documentation fields must be filled in every Cargo.toml
5. Publishing order must respect dependency graph: fm-core first, then fm-parser, etc.

## Scope
1. Audit each crate's Cargo.toml for crates.io readiness (name uniqueness, required fields, dependency types).
2. Choose crate names (e.g., frankenmermaid-core, frankenmermaid-parser, etc. — or use fm- prefix as-is if available).
3. Check name availability on crates.io for all 8 crate names.
4. Add missing fields (description, documentation, homepage, repository, keywords, categories) to each Cargo.toml.
5. Create a publishing script or document the manual publish order.
6. Test with `cargo publish --dry-run` for each crate.

## Dependencies
- FNX integration must be cleanly optional (it already is via feature flags, but verify no compile failures with default features on a clean checkout).

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo publish --dry-run` succeeds for all 8 crates.
- Publishing order documented.
- Names reserved on crates.io (or verified available).

+{"id":"bd-b3h0","title":"Prepare crates.io publishing strategy for workspace crates","description":"## Problem
README admits: "frankenmermaid is not published on crates.io yet, so cargo install frankenmermaid is not a working install path today." This is honest but represents a product gap. Users who expect cargo install will be disappointed.

## Challenges
The workspace has 8 crates (fm-core, fm-parser, fm-layout, fm-render-svg, fm-render-term, fm-render-canvas, fm-wasm, fm-cli). Publishing to crates.io requires:
1. Each crate must have unique names (fm-core might conflict; may need frankenmermaid-core etc.)
2. Dependencies between crates must use crates.io versions, not path dependencies
3. The FNX git dependency (franken_networkx) must be optional and not required for the default publish
4. License, description, documentation fields must be filled in every Cargo.toml
5. Publishing order must respect dependency graph: fm-core first, then fm-parser, etc.

## Scope
1. Audit each crate's Cargo.toml for crates.io readiness (name uniqueness, required fields, dependency types).
2. Choose crate names (e.g., frankenmermaid-core, frankenmermaid-parser, etc. — or use fm- prefix as-is if available).
3. Check name availability on crates.io for all 8 crate names.
4. Add missing fields (description, documentation, homepage, repository, keywords, categories) to each Cargo.toml.
5. Create a publishing script or document the manual publish order.
6. Test with `cargo publish --dry-run` for each crate.

## Dependencies
- FNX integration must be cleanly optional (it already is via feature flags, but verify no compile failures with default features on a clean checkout).

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo publish --dry-run` succeeds for all 8 crates.
- Publishing order documented.
- Names reserved on crates.io (or verified available).
- README updated with `cargo install frankenmermaid` (or whatever the final binary crate name is).","notes":"Blocker: franken-kernel is a git dependency used by fm-core. Either publish franken-kernel to crates.io first, or vendor its code. Also need to add metadata (description, repository, etc.) to all crate Cargo.tomls.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:26:23.518446553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T23:53:59.027739817Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b3h0","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:06.019576375Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b3h0","depends_on_id":"bd-w9id","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:26:39.601126552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b65r","title":"Add comprehensive classDef/style test suite","description":"## Problem
classDef/style is getting wired (bd-65l0) but needs dedicated test coverage to prevent regression.

## Scope
1. **Parser tests**: Verify classDef definitions with various CSS properties are correctly stored in IR.
2. **Render tests**: Verify classDef produces correct SVG attributes/styles.
3. **Precedence tests**: theme defaults < classDef < style directive < init override.
4. **Edge cases**: empty classDef, classDef with quoted values, multiple classes on one node, classDef applied to nodes that don't exist.
5. **Golden test**: flowchart_with_styles.mmd exercising classDef + style + multiple classes.

## Dependencies
- bd-65l0 (classDef wiring) should be done first, but parser tests can be written independently.

## Acceptance Criteria
- 10+ unit tests covering classDef parsing, rendering, and precedence.
- 1 golden test for styled flowchart.
- Regression-safe: any future change to classDef handling will break a test.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:26:48.968254255Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:06.567546092Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b65r","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:06.566949423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b65r","depends_on_id":"bd-65l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:03.073111246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b6gg","title":"Implement accDescription and accTitle accessibility directives","description":"# Accessibility Directives

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
accTitle: Process Flow
accDescr: This diagram shows the order processing pipeline from receipt to delivery.
accDescr {
  Multi-line description
  of the diagram's purpose
}
\`\`\`
- accTitle: sets the accessible title for screen readers (maps to SVG <title> element)
- accDescr: sets the accessible description (maps to SVG <desc> element)
- Both are global, apply to the entire diagram
- Used by all diagram types

## Current State
- accTitle/accDescr are NOT parsed
- SVG renderer already has accessibility support (a11y) but doesn't read these from IR

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Recognize accTitle and accDescr (single-line and multi-line block) directives. Store in MermaidDiagramMeta.
2. **IR**: Add accessible_title: Option<String> and accessible_description: Option<String> to MermaidDiagramMeta.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Emit <title> and <desc> elements within the root <svg> element.
4. **Tests**: Single-line accTitle, accDescr, multi-line accDescr block, SVG output verification.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:12.842946048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T05:17:20.129238031Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T05:17:20.128753604Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":174,"issue_id":"bd-b6gg","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:22Z"},{"id":194,"issue_id":"bd-b6gg","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. accTitle: 'Process Flow' → verify <title> element in SVG root
2. accDescr: 'Single line description' → verify <desc> element in SVG root
3. accDescr { multi-line } → verify multi-line <desc>
4. Both accTitle and accDescr → verify both elements present
5. Neither present → verify no empty <title>/<desc> elements
6. Works with flowchart, sequence, class, state → verify across diagram types","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24Z"}]}
